flashcards.definition: 1980–2015 policy limiting most families to one child; reduced TFR but caused demographic imbalance.
Indian state that achieved low fertility (~1.6 TFR) through female education and healthcare, without coercion.
Government policies encouraging higher birth rates (e.g., France, Sweden, South Korea offering incentives).
Government policies discouraging births/reducing population growth (e.g., China's one-child policy).
Education, economic opportunities, and reproductive rights for women — the most effective way to reduce fertility.
